title: thelypteris tetragona var. guadalupensis
intro: Thelypteris tetragona var. guadalupensis, also known as Guadalupe maidenhair fern, is a rare and endemic variety of the maidenhair fern species found in limited regions of the Guadalupe Mountains in New Mexico and Texas. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Thelypteris tetragona var. guadalupensis are believed to include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as treatment of skin conditions like eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant may include teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of these uses.
